title: acciddasuite
text: acciddasuite builds infectious disease forecasts three steps: get_data() check_data(): fetch validate surveillance data. get_ncast() (optional): correct recent weeks reporting delays. get_fcast(): evaluate models via cross-validation forecast future. package relies fable modeling framework follows standard forecasting workflow described Hyndman & Athanasopoulos (2021). overall goal provide public health professionals easily-adoptable approach generating ensemble outputs statistical models, evaluating forecasts, visualizing outputs.

title: acciddasuite
text: get information know whether forecasting best approach task, follow steps article.

title: acciddasuite
text: fetch weekly COVID-19 hospital admissions New York CDC NHSN via epidatr. Setting revisions = TRUE retrieves full revision history (.e. past versions data), needed nowcasting. get_data() returns validated accidda_data object: can also bring data. Just pass check_data(). See vignette("external_data") formatting details.
code: library(acciddasuite) df <- get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_value = "ny", revisions = TRUE) df #> <accidda_data> #>  #> Location: NY  #> Target:   wk inc covid hosp  #> Window:   2020-08-08 to 2026-04-11 ( 297 dates ) #> History:  TRUE ( 2024-11-17 to 2026-04-12 )

title: acciddasuite
text: recent weeks surveillance data almost always low hospitals still filing late reports (right truncated). feed raw counts forecaster, predictions biased downward. get_ncast() estimates recent counts look like reports arrive. default max_delay = 4, last 4 weeks corrected; everything left untouched.  corrected ncast$data contains two extra columns: ncast_lower ncast_upper (95% CrI) corrected weeks. get_fcast() detects automatically uses propagate nowcasting uncertainty final forecast.
code: ncast <- get_ncast(df) ncast #> <accidda_ncast> #>  #> Nowcasted 4 weeks: 2026-03-21 to 2026-04-11  #>  #> $data  corrected series (297 rows) #> $plot  nowcast visualisation ncast$plot

title: acciddasuite
text: get_fcast() two things: Model selection: time series cross-validation full (median corrected) series, starting eval_start_date. Models ranked WIS; best top_n form equal weight ensemble. Final forecast: projects h weeks future. nowcast columns present, forecast produced three baselines (lower, median, upper nowcast estimates) pooled, prediction intervals reflect model uncertainty nowcast uncertainty. set eval_start_date mark start evaluation window. least 52 weeks data must precede date. Default models : SNAIVE (Seasonal Naïve): Assumes week look like week last year. simplest possible baseline. ETS (Exponential Smoothing): weighted average recent weeks matter older ones. Adapts trends seasonal patterns. THETA: Splits data long-term trend short-term fluctuations, forecasts separately, combines . ARIMA: Learns repeating patterns past values predict future ones. Auto-configured find best fit. use pipetime::time_pipe() log long model selection forecasting steps take.  View forecast evaluation viewing score element object:
code: eval_start_date <- max(ncast$data$target_end_date) - 28 fcast <- get_fcast(   ncast,   eval_start_date = eval_start_date,   top_n = 4,   h = 4 ) |>    pipetime::time_pipe("base fcast", log = "log")  fcast #> <accidda_fcast> #>  #> Models evaluated: #>  model_id       wis #>    <char>     <num> #>       ETS  30.16633 #>     THETA  41.34537 #>  ENSEMBLE  79.64422 #>    SNAIVE 297.35408 #>  #> Forecast horizon: #>   From: 2026-03-14  #>   To:   2026-05-09  #>  #> Contents: #>   $hubcast   hub forecast object #>   $score     model ranking table #>   $plot      ggplot2 object fcast$plot fcast$score #> Key: <model_id> #>    model_id       wis interval_coverage_50 interval_coverage_95 #>      <char>     <num>                <num>                <num> #> 1:      ETS  30.16633                  1.0                    1 #> 2:    THETA  41.34537                  0.5                    1 #> 3: ENSEMBLE  79.64422                  0.5                    1 #> 4:   SNAIVE 297.35408                  0.0                    1 #>    wis_relative_skill #>                 <num> #> 1:          0.4091929 #> 2:          0.5608316 #> 3:          1.0803386 #> 4:          4.0334765

title: acciddasuite
text: model compatible fable framework can passed via extra_models: can check long step took calling pipetime::get_log():
code: library(fable) library(fable.prophet) extra <- list(   CUSTOM_ARIMA = ARIMA(observation ~ pdq(1,1,0)),   PROPHET = prophet(observation ~ season("year")),   EPIESTIM = EPIESTIM(observation, mean_si = 3, std_si = 2, rt_window = 7) )  fcast <- get_fcast(   ncast,   eval_start_date = eval_start_date,   top_n = 4,   h = 3,   extra_models = extra ) |>    pipetime::time_pipe("extra fcast", log = "log") pipetime::get_log() #> $log #>             timestamp       label duration unit #> 1 2026-04-24 16:51:52  base fcast 18.83492 secs #> 2 2026-04-24 16:52:15 extra fcast 25.72153 secs

title: acciddasuite
text: RespiLens platform sharing respiratory disease forecasts. Use to_respilens() export forecast JSON upload MyRespiLens.
code: to_respilens(fcast, "respilens.json")

title: Preparing External Data
text: can use surveillance dataset acciddasuite — just pass check_data() validate enter pipeline.

title: Preparing External Data
text: data frame must 4 columns: enable nowcasting (correcting reporting delays), add 5th column: as_of, target_end_date can appear multiple times (one row per revision). Without , target_end_date appear .

title: Preparing External Data
text: check_data() output tells whether revision history available. can go directly forecasting: , data revision history, nowcast first:
code: library(acciddasuite) head(df) ##   target_end_date observation location             target ## 1      2024-01-01          13       NY inc hosp influenza ## 2      2024-01-08          15       NY inc hosp influenza ## 3      2024-01-15          19       NY inc hosp influenza ## 4      2024-01-22          22       NY inc hosp influenza ## 5      2024-01-29          25       NY inc hosp influenza ## 6      2024-02-05          11       NY inc hosp influenza checked <- check_data(df) checked ## <accidda_data> ##  ## Location: NY  ## Target:   inc hosp influenza  ## Window:   2024-01-01 to 2024-12-23 ( 52 dates ) ## History:  FALSE get_fcast(checked, eval_start_date = "2024-11-01", h = 4) checked |> get_ncast() |> get_fcast(eval_start_date = "2024-11-01", h = 4)

title: Disease Forecast Planning for Public Health
text: Disease forecasting important tool public health, however, maximize utility tools, thoughtful planning required clearly define approach problem needing addressed. ACCIDDA Forecast Suite provides comprehensive toolkit building infectious disease forecasting pipelines hubs, discussions planning prior starting forecasting project important. provide series questions consider addressing prior using package. package suite still active development, welcome contributions feedback community.

what: Step 1: Why are we interested in forecasting?
title: Disease Forecast Planning for Public Health
text: First, needs clearly defined project get started. set questions consider begin process: question attempting answer? insights hope gain? Determine trying gain forecasting project. specific useful determining approach. audience benefit insights? Documenting use forecasts assist interpretation forecasting output. far future interested forecasting? far future insights need useful? aspects forecast need accurate? Clearly defining think going useful information determine approach problems forecasting best tool. example, seasonal influenza forecasting typically involves predictions 1 4 weeks future. Forecasts provide range possible trajectories time period. Predicting specific number, number total hospitalizations time period require different approach models. Answer prior proceeding: defined forecasting project approach?
code: - YES → Proceed to next steps  - NO  → Continue defining the approach

title: Disease Forecast Planning for Public Health
text: Next, defining pathogen, target (time series data), geographical area, time resolution order know data required forecasting project. pathogen(s) interested ? straight-forward possibly answered step , ensuring clear pathogen (e.g., influenza, COVID-19, RSV, etc) forecasted important. outcomes measure pathogen? outcomes relevant questions, insights, audience? outcomes also referred target. importantly, timely target reported? timing target adequate enough updating forecasts evaluation? Determining specifically forecasted important data collection steps. examples forecasting targets: spatial unit provide best insight? data available scale? may include, limited : time resolution adaquate available provide required information audience? Planning time steps important determining data consistently readily available resolution. Also, assist thinking reporting delays lag time time steps. typical forecasting time steps include days, weeks, even months. Answer prior proceeding: defined pathogen, target, spatial unit, time resolution?
code: - Reported cases of influenza - COVID-19 Hospitalizations - Respiratory disease deaths - Emergency Department (ED) visits related to respiratory disease-like illnesses - State, county, city, health jurisdiction, hospital system, or even facility (e.g., hospital) - YES → Proceed to next steps  - NO  → Continue defining these data elements

what: A suite of tools for epidemic forecasting
title: A suite of tools for epidemic forecasting
text: acciddasuite provides simple pipeline infectious diseases forecasts. validates input data (check_data()), optionally applies nowcasting adjust reporting delays (get_ncast()), generates forecasts (get_fcast()).

title: A suite of tools for epidemic forecasting
text: can install development version acciddasuite GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") #pak::pak("ACCIDDA/acciddasuite")

title: A suite of tools for epidemic forecasting
text: Save myRespiLens format:
code: library(acciddasuite) head(example_data) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   as_of      location target            target_end_date observation #>   <date>     <chr>    <chr>             <date>                <dbl> #> 1 2024-11-17 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #> 2 2024-11-24 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #> 3 2024-12-01 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #> 4 2024-12-08 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #> 5 2024-12-15 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #> 6 2024-12-22 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 fcast <- example_data |>   check_data() |>    get_ncast() |>    get_fcast(     eval_start_date = max(example_data$target_end_date) - 28,     h = 3 # forecast 3 weeks into the future   ) #> ℹ Using max_delay = 12 from data #> ℹ Truncating from max_delay = 12 to 4. #> Warning: 1 error encountered for ARIMA #> [1] #> ℹ Some rows containing NA values may be removed. This is fine if not #> unexpected. #> ℹ Some rows containing NA values may be removed. This is fine if not #> unexpected. #> Warning: 1 error encountered for ARIMA #> [1]  #>  #> 1 error encountered for ARIMA #> [1]  #>  #> 1 error encountered for ARIMA #> [1] fcast$plot to_respilens(fcast, path = "respilens.json")

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: Estimates current reproduction number (Rt) using EpiEstim simulates future incidence using projections. Works regular aggregation period integrates fable workflow via model().

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
code: EPIESTIM(   formula,   mean_si,   std_si,   rt_window = 14L,   n_sim = 100L,   R_fix_within = TRUE )

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: formula Response variable, e.g. observation. Exogenous regressors supported. mean_si Mean serial interval days. std_si Standard deviation serial interval days. rt_window Sliding window width days Rt estimation (dt_out EpiEstim). Controls many recent days inform current Rt. Smaller values track recent trends closely; larger values give smoother estimate. Defaults 14 days (~2 weeks). n_sim Number simulation paths forecast distribution. R_fix_within TRUE, Rt held constant within simulated path (recommended short horizons).

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: model definition use inside model().

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: aggregation period detected automatically tsibble index. recent data (rt_window estimation period plus serial interval period) passed EpiEstim's expectation-maximisation (EM) algorithm, reconstructs daily incidence aggregated counts estimating Rt rolling rt_window-day windows. recent Rt estimate used project forward via stochastic simulation. Forecasts returned sample distributions (one per horizon period) drawn n_sim epidemic paths.

what: Validate surveillance data for the acciddasuite pipeline — check_data
title: Validate surveillance data for the acciddasuite pipeline — check_data
text: Checks data frame required columns structure use get_ncast get_fcast. Returns typed accidda_data object downstream functions can accept without repeating validation.

title: Validate surveillance data for the acciddasuite pipeline — check_data
code: check_data(df)

title: Validate surveillance data for the acciddasuite pipeline — check_data
text: df data frame (tibble) least: target_end_date (Date), observation (numeric), location (character), target (character). optional as_of (Date) column enables nowcasting via get_ncast.

title: Validate surveillance data for the acciddasuite pipeline — check_data
text: accidda_data object (list) : data validated data frame coerced date types. location Single location identifier. target Single target identifier. window Named vector dates. history Logical. TRUE revision history (as_of) present.

title: Validate surveillance data for the acciddasuite pipeline — check_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # From get_data df <- get_data("covid", "ny") |> check_data()  # User-provided data my_df <- read.csv("my_data.csv") |> check_data()  # Then into the pipeline df |> get_fcast(eval_start_date = "2025-01-01") } # }

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
text: Fetch confirmed US hospital admissions NHSN source COVID-19, influenza, RSV using pub_covidcast.

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
code: get_data(pathogen, geo_value, revisions = FALSE)

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
text: pathogen Character. One "covid", "flu" "rsv". geo_value Character vector. Geographic value per pub_covidcast filter . revisions Logical. TRUE, fetches available revision history, producing data ready get_ncast. Default FALSE (latest version ).

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
text: accidda_data object (see check_data).

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
text: default latest version observation returned. Set revisions = TRUE retrieve available revision history, needed get_ncast.

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
code: get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_value = "ny") #> Warning: No API key found. You will be limited to non-complex queries and encounter rate #> limits if you proceed. #> ℹ See `?save_api_key()` for details on obtaining and setting API keys. #> This warning is displayed once every 8 hours. #> <accidda_data> #>  #> Location: NY  #> Target:   wk inc covid hosp  #> Window:   2020-08-08 to 2026-04-11 ( 297 dates ) #> History:  FALSE  # Fetch revision history for nowcasting get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_value = "ca", revisions = TRUE) #> <accidda_data> #>  #> Location: CA  #> Target:   wk inc covid hosp  #> Window:   2020-08-08 to 2026-04-11 ( 297 dates ) #> History:  TRUE ( 2024-11-17 to 2026-04-12 )

what: Forecast and evaluate time series models — get_fcast
title: Forecast and evaluate time series models — get_fcast
text: Selects evaluates multiple time series models via expanding-window cross-validation, produces final forward-looking forecast.

title: Forecast and evaluate time series models — get_fcast
code: get_fcast(df, eval_start_date, h = 4, top_n = 3, extra_models = NULL)

title: Forecast and evaluate time series models — get_fcast
text: df accidda_ncast object get_ncast, accidda_data object check_data get_data. underlying data frame contains ncast_lower ncast_upper columns, nowcast uncertainty propagation enabled automatically. eval_start_date Date string coercible Date. First date forecasts evaluated. least 52 weeks data must precede date. h Integer. Forecast horizon weeks. Default 4. top_n Integer. Number top ranked models included ensemble. Default 3. extra_models Named list additional forecasting models passed fabletools::model(). element must reference observation column compatible fabletools::forecast(). Custom models can constructed using fable modelling framework, see fabletools extension_models vignette. name list element used model label output.

title: Forecast and evaluate time series models — get_fcast
text: object class accidda_fcast containing: hubcast Hub-format forecast object (model_out_tbl oracle_output). score Model ranking based rolling origin WIS. plot ggplot2 object showing forecasts prediction intervals.

title: Forecast and evaluate time series models — get_fcast
text: Cross-validation. eval_start_date onwards, models repeatedly refitted data cutoff used forecast next h weeks. Models ranked WIS; best top_n form equal-weight ensemble. Nowcast uncertainty. accidda_ncast object supplied (accidda_data whose data frame contains ncast_lower ncast_upper columns), cross-validation runs observation column (median corrected series). final forecast produced three times (median, lower 95\ resulting distributions pooled, prediction intervals reflect model uncertainty nowcast uncertainty.

title: Forecast and evaluate time series models — get_fcast
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ extra_models <- list(   CUSTOM_ARIMA = ARIMA(observation ~ pdq(1, 1, 0)),   PROPHET = fable.prophet::PROPHET(observation),   EPIESTIM = EPIESTIM(observation, mean_si = 3.96, std_si = 4.75) )  get_fcast(   df,   eval_start_date = "2025-01-01",   h = 4,   top_n = 3,   extra_models = extra_models ) } # }

title: Nowcast right-truncated surveillance data — get_ncast
text: recent weeks surveillance data almost always incomplete reporting delays (right truncation). get_ncast uses baselinenowcast estimate counts look like reports arrive.

title: Nowcast right-truncated surveillance data — get_ncast
code: get_ncast(df, max_delay = 4, draws = 1000, prop_delay = 0.5, scale_factor = 3)

title: Nowcast right-truncated surveillance data — get_ncast
text: df accidda_data object check_data get_data. Must revision history ($history == TRUE); use get_data(revisions = TRUE). max_delay Integer. Maximum reporting delay weeks. Default 4. draws Integer. Number posterior samples. Default 1000. prop_delay Numeric 0-1. Proportion reference times used delay estimation. Default 0.5. scale_factor Numeric. Multiplicative factor maximum delay estimation window. Default 3.

title: Nowcast right-truncated surveillance data — get_ncast
text: accidda_ncast object (list) : data Corrected time series. observation column contains nowcast median corrected weeks. Two extra columns, ncast_lower ncast_upper (95\ used get_fcast propagate nowcast uncertainty. plot ggplot2 visualisation nowcast correction.

title: Nowcast right-truncated surveillance data — get_ncast
text: default max_delay = 4, last 4 weeks treated right-truncated replaced nowcast estimates. Everything left untouched. function returns three corrected versions full series (nowcast median, lower, upper 95\ get_fcast can propagate nowcast uncertainty final forward-looking forecast.

title: Nowcast right-truncated surveillance data — get_ncast
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df    <- get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_value = "ca", revisions = TRUE) ncast <- get_ncast(df) fcast <- get_fcast(ncast, eval_start_date = "2025-01-01") } # }

title: Convert accidda_fcast to RespiLens format — to_respilens
text: accidda_fcast object class accidda_fcast, output get_fcast(). path Optional file path write JSON output . Must end .json. NULL, output written disk.

title: Convert accidda_fcast to RespiLens format — to_respilens
text: named list single metadata JSON structure one JSON structure per location.
